Response from Service ticket.
To my knowledge, there isn't yet a way to directly access your Adobe data within Tableau. However, some connectors have been developed to facilitate this process.
Daexus is a free connector (https://www.daexus.io/products/) that runs locally on your machine, ensuring that no third-party servers access your data, enhancing security. While I'm unsure whether it's Walmart-approved, I recommend contacting IT department for confirmation before using it. Himalay Patel on 16/Feb/24 10:22 AM

Event Criteria - the information you normally need or want to take about each event. 
•	Event Name – This will be assigned at the beginning of each event.  For Hurricanes it will start when the storm is named.
•	Store – Will need update each store individually
•	Event Start Date – Will use the date that we are first activated
•	Event End Date – Will use the date of the last day of activation
•	Event Type (hurricane, tornado, etc) 
o	Hurricane
o	Tornado
o	Severe Weather
o	Flood
o	Wildfire
o	Fire
o	Arson
o	Winter Weather
o	Civil Unrest
o	Protest
o	Loss of Infrastructure
o	Police Activity
o	Volcano

Formula:
Striving for excellence is a   core value at Walmart, and one of the ways we bring that to life is through   partnership and accountability between associates and managers. Associates   set, update, and work toward their goals, while managers provide feedback,   coach, manage performance, and build future capabilities. Working together,   associates and managers help drive our culture of growth and development to   help grow great careers. 
 
Review Cycle Starts Nov. 1 
Our annual Performance and   Pay review cycle begins Nov. 1, during which your manager will evaluate your   performance, and you’ll conclude with a discussion about your performance   rating and any pay changes. Here’s what to expect in the coming months and atoolkit of support resources: 

   
How to PrepareReview your progress toward your goals with your manager by Oct.        31 and make        any changes in the Performance & Pay tool before managers start        performance evaluations on Nov. 1. 

Update Goals   Attend a learning session between Nov. 6 - Jan. 13. Join one of seven sessions        designed to strengthen your skills and confidence in the performance and        pay process. You'll learn practical tips for completing your        self-evaluation and guidance to prepare for meaningful conversations        with your manager. See the options below and click the link to add a        session to your calendar. All times are in CT. November: 6th at 1 p.m.,11th at 7:30 a.m.,12th         at 1p.m. (Spanish)December: 4th at 1 p.m.,11th at 11 p.m.January: 13th at 8:30 a.m.,13th at 11 p.m.Complete your self-evaluation between Nov. 1 - Jan. 23. Use this opportunity to showcase        your impact, share your perspective, and tell your story. Your input        helps shape how your performance is seen.
 
We'll keep you updated   throughout the cycle with reminders and next steps. For questions, reach out   to your manager or people partner.
